name: Aspen Technology (AspenONE) API
what_it_does: Aspen Technology (AspenONE) REST APIs for industrial AI and process optimization software. Enables AI agents to manage process simulation model execution and result retrieval for chemical and refinery engineering, handle real-time process optimization (RTO) data and setpoint recommendations for production optimization, access asset performance management (APM) and equipment reliability prediction data, retrieve process historian data and statistical analytics for operational performance, manage process safety analysis and LOPA (Layer of Protection Analysis) data, handle supply chain optimization and production planning data for industrial planning, access energy optimization and utility cost reduction recommendation data, retrieve advanced process control (APC) performance monitoring and constraint data, manage refinery LP (Linear Programming) optimization model data for crude oil and production planning, and integrate AspenONE data with DCS, historian, and enterprise planning systems.
use_cases: Retrieving process simulation results from engineering optimization agents
Accessing RTO setpoint recommendations from production optimization agents
Monitoring APM equipment health from reliability engineering agents
Analyzing refinery LP plans from production planning agents
not_for: Non-process industries without chemical, refinery, or petrochemical engineering
Simple manufacturing MES without process simulation and optimization
Commercial building automation without industrial process engineering context
best_when: An oil refinery, chemical plant, or petrochemical company using AspenONE wants AI agents to automate process simulation data retrieval, production optimization recommendations, equipment reliability monitoring, and supply chain planning.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: AspenONE models include process safety analysis (LOPA, HAZOP support) — automated safety analysis interpretation requires certified process safety engineer review. Real-time process optimization setpoints affect production unit operations; automated setpoint implementation without process control engineer validation creates process safety risk. Asset performance management failure predictions require mechanical integrity engineer verification before maintenance action.

auth.notes: Aspen Technology uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Developer resources at aspentech.com. Aspen Mtell (APM) and AspenONE Engineering have separate API surfaces. Cloud and on-premise deployment. No webhooks — event integration via process historian or SCADA. Python SDK available. Bedford, Massachusetts HQ. Acquired by Emerson Electric (2022).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual aspenONE subscription; module licensing (engineering, manufacturing, energy, chemicals)
pricing.notes: Bedford, Massachusetts. Founded 1981. Acquired by Emerson Electric (2022). Industrial AI and optimization software. 2,300+ customers in chemical, refinery, and energy industries. Aspen Plus and HYSYS process simulators are industry standards. Aspen Mtell AI-based APM. Emerson acquisition expands process control integration. Competes with AVEVA for industrial software market.

performance.rate_limits: No public rate limits — compute-intensive operations have variable latency

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: Process simulation and RTO setpoint data affects live production units; never implement automated setpoints without process control engineer approval and DCS interlock verification
Simulation latency — Aspen Plus and HYSYS process simulation runs are compute-intensive; simulation API calls may have 30 second to several minute execution time
Product suite fragmentation — AspenONE includes 50+ separate applications; Aspen Plus (engineering), HYSYS (oil/gas), Mtell (APM), PIMS (refinery planning) have separate APIs
Emerson acquisition — Aspentech acquired by Emerson (2022); product roadmap integrating with Emerson DeltaV DCS; verify current API direction and integration approach
On-premise engineering simulation — most Aspen Plus/HYSYS deployments are on-premise Windows; cloud simulation requires Aspen Technology cloud deployment
Refinery LP optimization — PIMS LP optimization models are production-critical; changes to LP model data or constraints require process engineer and commercial review
agent_readiness.security_notes: Industrial AI and process optimization. SOC2, IEC61511. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Process simulation and production data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Process optimization and industrial AI. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Process simulation and production data.
